title: Mega Sharpedo Battle Guide
summary: A fast [Water]/[Dark] physical attacker with [strong-jaw] — [crunch], [ice-fang], and [psychic-fangs] all hit 1.5x harder.
strengths: 140 Attack + 105 Speed is strong offensive spread
weaknesses: Defenses of 70/70 fold to priority moves

recommendedItems.reason: Mandatory Mega Stone with no alternative

bodyMarkdown:
## Overview
Mega Sharpedo is a fast [Water]/[Dark] physical attacker whose ability [strong-jaw] boosts all biting moves by 50%. Running three different fang moves gives it a tight but wide coverage spread and lets it threaten most of the metagame in exchange for its notoriously fragile defenses.

## Strengths
[strong-jaw] turns [crunch] into an effective ~120 BP STAB, [ice-fang] into [Dragon]/[Grass]/[Flying]/[Ground] coverage, and [psychic-fangs] into a [Fighting]/[Poison] check that also breaks [light-screen] and [reflect]. With 140 Attack and 105 Speed, Mega Sharpedo outpaces most neutral threats in the 100s tier.

## Weaknesses & Answers
70/70 physical bulk is very fragile and priority moves like [bullet-punch] and [mach-punch] often one-shot it. Five weaknesses — [Fighting], [Electric], [Grass], [Fairy], [Bug] — make predicting switch-ins risky. The Mega Evolution turn loses the base [speed-boost] opportunity, so the first turn is slower than expected.

## Recommended Moveset
[crunch] is the STAB you always click. [ice-fang] and [psychic-fangs] flex the coverage spread and both benefit from [strong-jaw]. [protect] is mandatory in doubles for Mega safety and partner setup. [waterfall] is tempting but loses the [strong-jaw] boost, so fang coverage is generally better.

## Item · Ability · Tera
[sharpedonite] is mandatory. [strong-jaw] replaces the base [speed-boost]. Tera [Dark] stacks on the [crunch] plan, while Tera [Water] boosts [waterfall] if you run it and patches the [Electric] weakness by shifting types defensively.

## Teammates & Synergy
Pair with [tailwind] setters to maintain speed control and [fake-out] users to buy setup windows. Cover the [Fighting] and [Electric] weaknesses with [Psychic] and [Ground] partners. [intimidate] support mitigates the paper bulk.

## Play Patterns
Protect on the Mega turn to avoid damage, then begin cycling fang moves based on match-up. Commit to [crunch] into neutral targets, pivot to [ice-fang] for [Dragon]/[Flying], and use [psychic-fangs] to break screens or deal with [Fighting]. Tera on a predicted priority move to survive and KO.
